heroHeadline: Somalia: NISA Kills 33 Al-Shabaab in Middle Shabelle; AFRICOM 57th Strike Near Kismayo; President Inaugurates Blue Economy Ministry as May 15 Constitutional Deadline Looms
heroSubtitle: NISA and international partners killed 33 Al-Shabaab fighters in a two-phase precision raid on Guulane, Middle Shabelle, on April 22 — one of the largest single intelligence-led operations of 2026. AFRICOM conducted its 57th Somalia airstrike of 2026 on April 21 near Wadajir (~90km SW of Kismayo), maintaining the unprecedented 200+/year pace. On April 23, President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud inaugurated the new Ministry of Ports and Marine Transport, advancing his 'blue economy revolution' as Turkish drillship Çağrı Bey continues drilling the Curad-1 offshore oil well — Somalia's first offshore exploratory well. The constitutional crisis deepens: 22 days remain before Mohamud's presidential term expires (May 15), with the opposition declaring he loses legitimacy after that date, Puntland having declared a 'constitutional vacuum,' and mass defections of senior officials to the opposition. The SNA/Macawisley counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal (seized by Al-Shabaab on April 19) remains in mobilization phase. Somalia faces its most dangerous convergence of governance failure, security deterioration, and humanitarian catastrophe since 2021.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
